author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
hari he!
bhramite saṁsāra⋅vane, kabhu daiva⋅saŋghaṭane,
konamate kona bhāgyavān
tava pada uddeśiyā, thāke kṛtāñjali hañā,
ekabāra ohe bhagavān

seikṣaṇe tā’ra jata, amaṅgala haya hata,
sumaṅgala haya puṣṭa ati
āra nāhi kṣaya haya, krame tā’ra śubhodaya,
tā’re deya sarvottama⋅gati

emana dayālu tumi, emana durbhāgā āmi,
kabhu nā karinu paraṇāma
tava pāda⋅padma⋅prati, nā jāne e duṣṭamati,
bhaktivinodera pariṇāma
en_translation:
O Hari! While wandering in the forest of material existence, by some divine arrangement, some fortunate soul, somehow aiming for Your feet, stands with folded hands just once, O Bhagavān!

At that very moment, all his misfortunes are destroyed, and great auspiciousness becomes extremely strong. There is no more loss for him; gradually his good fortune appears, granting him the supreme destination.

You are so merciful, and I am so unfortunate; I never offered my obeisances to Your lotus feet. This wicked mind does not know what will become of Bhaktivinoda.
